canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, TheoremQA is consistently characterized as a benchmark used to assess mathematical reasoning in language models. The benchmark is defined as containing university-level STEM questions, with one source specifying it includes 800 problems from fields like math, physics, and engineering that require the application of mathematical theorems. Its primary function is to measure mathematical reasoning, a use case reinforced by multiple papers that group it with other mathematics-focused benchmarks such as MATH, GSM-Plus, SVAMP, and ASDiv. A less common application mentioned in one paper is its use as an out-of-domain test set.
papers: Advancing LLM Reasoning Generalists with Preference Trees
BRIGHT: A Realistic and Challenging Benchmark for Reasoning-Intensive Retrieval
DOTS: Learning to Reason Dynamically in LLMs via Optimal Reasoning Trajectories Search
Latent Space Chain-of-Embedding Enables Output-free LLM Self-Evaluation
Token Assorted: Mixing Latent and Text Tokens for Improved Language Model Reasoning
